wikidata_candidates.label: Clemente Canepari
wikidata_candidates.reasoning: The two records describe completely different individuals. The card describes John E. Canepari, an American civil engineer working for the U.S. Coast Guard in New York. The Wikidata entry describes Clemente Canepari, an Italian professional cyclist who lived from 1886 to 1966. Name, occupation, and geographic location all differ significantly.

results.possibleIdentity: John E. Canepari was a civil engineer active in the late 1960s and early 1970s. During this time, he was employed by the United States Coast Guard (USCG) and stationed at Governors Island, New York. His technical expertise spanned the fields of civil engineering, structural engineering, and hydraulics. Canepari was an engineer-member of the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization founded to facilitate collaborations between the artistic and technical communities. He participated in E.A.T.'s Technical Services Program, which maintained index cards of engineers and scientists interested in working with artists. In his later career, he became a Senior Vice President at PACO Group, Inc., a project management and construction consulting firm with offices in New York and Florida. He was a professional in the field of construction and project management, contributing to the Project Management Institute (PMI) and authoring technical papers on topics such as configuration management in large-scale infrastructure projects.
